Because that solves the problem created by the first problem, which is like so much business that it's breaking our systems So this is a really good approach now one thing I will say about this as I've seen so so so many back-end programs Fail because they get very bottlenecked and positioned into we only want to solve that next problem and then so they they kind of Disqualify so many of their clients who would want to buy more stuff from them But don't feel like they're qualified for the next thing So if you have a very homogenous market like gyms like doctors like Kyra's where you can get them to like a certain activation point and They'll like the success rates really really high They all have x y and z result and then when they have that x y and z result they all need that next thing I think this is great But if you're selling a very kind of mixed bag market like coaches where there's all in all these different places I find that to be relatively difficult and you just kind of need to create something That's a little bit more broader on the back end as well Another thing you can offer is events retreats and masterminds are amazing for back-end Personally, that's something that we wanted to rope in for sales team accelerator and it'll be in the future just a matter of when and then you can also do a mixture of the above right You know the just know that the biggest drivers of back-end sales will be community culture and environment right?
